texts.full_text: or two independent random samples were selected from the target population and sample statistics (e.g., sample sizes, means, and standard deviations or sample sizes and proportions) were generated. Point estimates are the best single-valued estimates of an unknown population parameter. Because these can vary from sample to sample, most investigations start with a point estimate and build in a margin of error. The margin of error quantifies sampling variability and includes a value from the Z or t distribution reflecting the selected confidence level as well as the standard error of the point estimate. It is important to remember that the confidence interval contains a range of likely values for the unknown population parameter; a range of values for the population parameter consistent with the data. It is also possible, although the likelihood is small, that the confidence interval does not contain the true population parameter. This is important to remember in interpreting intervals. texts.full_text: main base in Istanbul, the Persians under Imam Kulu began to regain some of their former territories. Although an army from the Crimean Khanate (which was an Ottoman vassal) under Adil Giray was sent to the Caucasus in summer 1579 as a reinforcement, they were routed by Hamza Mirza and Mirza Salman, and Adil Giray was taken prisoner, and put in Qazvin, where he was later executed. Consequently, Osman Pasha had to retreat to North Caucasus. In 1582, the Ottoman Porte sent a second reinforcing force under Cafer Pasha, the governor of Caffa, to restore Ottoman dominance in the area. Battle The 50,000-strong Persians under Imam Kulu, together with Georgian irregulars, attacked again in the spring of 1583. The main body of the Persian army was divided into two parts: one part moved to Derbend and another part took positions at Alishar near Shemkara Pass (modern-day Dagestan). Meanwhile, Ottoman forces under Osman Pasha gathered in Tiflis for a counterattack. On April 7th, the Persians launched their first assault on the Ottomans. The battle unfolded mainly along two fronts: one at Derbend and another near Shemkara Pass. Both sides fought fiercely with heavy casualties. However, due to superior leadership and tactics by Osman Pasha, as well as effective use of cavalry and infantry in combination, the Ottomans managed to repel the Persian attacks. The decisive moment came during a night clash when the Ottoman forces used torches to illuminate their surroundings and surprise the Persians. This unexpected maneuver caused confusion

From 2002 to 2003 he was a member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee, vice chairman of the CPC Central Military Commission; director and secretary of Party committee of PLA General Armament Department. In March 2008, he was the director and secretary of Party committee of PLA General Armament Department. He was a member of the 15th CPC Central Committee, and was a member of the 16th CPC Central Committee and member of the 16th CPC Politburo. texts.full_text: pulmonary arterial endothelium in a novel rat model of venous thromboembolism. The experimental studies of venous thromboembolism (VTE) as an entity and the response of the pulmonary arterial endothelium after VTE are still rare. The objective of this study was to observe changes in the pulmonary arterial endothelium using a novel rat model of VTE. Rats were allocated to the VTE (n = 54) or control groups (n = 9). The left femoral vein was blocked using a microvessel clip to form deep vein thrombosis (DVT). One, four or seven-day-old thrombi were injected into the right femoral vein to induce DVT-pulmonary thromboembolism (DVT-PTE). The rats were sacrificed 1, 4 or 7 days later (D(n(1,4,7)) P(n(1,4,7)) subgroups (n = 6)), and the lungs were examined using light and electron microscopy. On gross dissection, the rate of DVT formation was higher on day 1 (D(1)P(n): 100%, 18/18) than day 4 (D(4)P(n): 83%, 15/18; χ(2) = 5.900, P = 0.015) or day 7 (D(7)P(n): 44%, 8/18; χ(2) = 13.846, P = 0.000). Not for IE6, cause selectboxes in IE6 are strange( maybe you can try to fix IE6 with some iframe hack); A: Fairly old question with some good suggestions, but none seem to directly answer the original question. In case anybody out there is wondering, I believe the OP was wanting to keep the visual appearance of the system/browser select element, but use his own custom drop-down menu instead of the system/browser drop-down menu. In this case, the onclick event will occur too late for you to stop the actual drop-down menu from displaying. What you want to do is bind to the mousedown event, and prevent the event from propagating to the default behavior: document.getElementById('my_select_id').onmousedown = function(event) { // ... do something here...perhaps display your own custom menu, an advanced selection chooser, focus another element, display a message, or some other custom handling. event.preventDefault(); // This prevents the drop-down menu from displaying } Notes: Replacing the drop-down with a custom-designed element (as suggested by others) isn't always an option. In some cases, you'll end up either having to completely omit default/system drop-downs from your site (in favor of a custom-designed element), or you have to live with a mismatch in visual appearance due to browser/system/theme differences (unless you feel like designing the custom element to match every conceivable visual aesthetic/theme.) texts.full_text: Regulation of Abd-B expression by Cyclin G and Corto in the abdominal epithelium of Drosophila. Polycomb-group (PcG) and trithorax-group (trxG) genes encode important regulators of homeotic genes, repressors and activators, respectively. They act through epigenetic mechanisms that maintain chromatin structure. The corto gene of Drosophila melanogaster encodes a co-factor of these regulators belonging to the Enhancer of Trithorax and Polycomb class. We have previously shown that Corto maintains the silencing of the homeotic gene Abdominal-B in the embryo and that it interacts with a cyclin, Cyclin G, suggesting that it could be a major actor in the connection between Polycomb/Trithorax function and the cell cycle. We show here that inactivation of Cyclin G by RNA interference leads to rotated genitalia and cuticle defects in the posterior abdomen of pupae and that corto genetically interacts with Cyclin G

Feelings of loneliness and “being left out” shot up after the iPhone came on the scene, with kids’ ability to witness, constantly, how much fun everyone else was (or at least seemed to be) having. Twenge notes that correlation is not causation, but nonetheless makes a persuasive case that kids spending more time on social media, rather than being out and about, are less happy than earlier generations, and also less happy than their current-day counterparts who get away from their screens to domore engaging activities. The introduction of the iPhone marked a significant turning point in how people interacted with technology and each other. Prior to its arrival, communication was more direct—phone calls, face-to-face meetings, and written letters—and the gradual rise in social interactions seemed more manageable through typical trend lines. However, once smartphones became ubiquitous, these trends shifted dramatically. The constant connectivity brought by apps like Facebook, Instagram, and Snapchat made it easy for individuals to constantly see others' highlights, which often led to feelings of inadequacy or loneliness among younger users. This visibility into the lives of their peers on social media platforms could be a stark reminder of what they perceived as missing from their own lives. While Jeneration researcher like Jean Twenge acknowledges that correlation does not imply causation, she argues convincingly that the increased screen time correlates with higher rates of mental health issues among young people. You want to re-upload the same file without having to delete it first, isn't that correct? One approach you could take is using the `fileInput` function with `prompt = FALSE`, but then also pass an extra argument `filename` and use Shiny's built-in UI element `actionButton`. When the button is clicked for "Upload again", set the input field to default or clear its value, then call fileInput on this field. Here's how you can implement it: ```python from PySide.QtGui import QApplication, QMainWindow, QMenu, QAction def main(): app = QApplication([]) # Create the main window window = QMainWindow() # Create a menu bar menu_bar = window.menuBar() # Add a menu to the menu bar file_menu = menu_bar.addMenu("File") # Create a checkable action toggle_action = QAction("Toggle Option", window) toggle_action.setCheckable(True) # Make the action checkable # Optionally, connect a slot to handle state changes if needed def on_toggle_state_changed(state): print(f"Toggle option is {'checked' if state else 'unchecked'}") toggle_action.toggled.connect(on_toggle_state_changed) # Add the action

Size exclusion chromatography using dextran standards was applied to determine the average molecular weight of the polysaccharide fractions obtained. The results indicated that the fraction SJZDP-I-I (351.0 kDa) has the highest Mw, followed by SJZDP-II-I (171.9 kDa) and SJZDP-I-II (16.9 kDa). The Mw of these three fractions were different from those of polysaccharides SJZPS-Vb-1 (38.3 kDa) and SJZPS-Vb-2 (28.0 kDa) as previously reported \[[@B23-molecules-22-01341]\]. 2.3. As shown in the table, fraction SJZDP-II-I contained linkage units typical of pectic polysaccharides. texts.full_text: re's how You can achieve this using SQL: 1. Hourly Interval: To fetch records for each hour, you can use the `HOUR()` function and then group by the hour. ```sql SELECT HOUR(timestamp_column) AS hour, COUNT(*) FROM your_table GROUP BY HOUR(timestamp_column); ``` 2. Daily Interval: For daily intervals, use the `DATE()` or `DAY()` function: ```sql -- Using DATE() function SELECT DATE(timestamp_column) AS day, COUNT(*) FROM your_table GROUP BY DATE(timestamp_column); -- Or using DAY() function (this will give you a number from 1 to 31) SELECT DAY(timestamp_column) AS day, COUNT(*) FROM your_table GROUP BY DAY(timestamp_column); ``` 3. Weekly Interval: To get records on a weekly basis, you can use the `WEEK()` function: ```sql SELECT WEEK(timestamp_column) AS week, COUNT(*) FROM your_table GROUP BY WEEK(timestamp_column); ``` Replace `your_table` with the actual name of your table and `timestamp_column` with the name of the column containing timestamps in your database. texts.full_text: who listens to a Walkman as he rides, around 8 a.m. Central time. He asked me if I'd heard that terrorists had crashed a plane into the World Trade Center. Dick's a guy who delivers jokes with a deadpan expression, and I was waiting for the punch line ... there was none. I'd only gotten five miles in, but I told Dick that I had to get to the office immediately. We were just a half mile from the newspaper, a half mile I covered with more urgency than usual. The newsroom was virtually deserted except for the features department. We started calling people in and I called the editor at home and ctold him what little we had so far. He said he'd be right in. Our initial idea was to put everything on our website as soon as possible, but we also realized that this was a story that might need all hands on deck. We began making calls to our staff photographers and journalists, wanting to round up as many men as we could to cover the story from different angles. I grabbed my camera gear, knowing that I would have to head out to Denver's airport or some other place to take photos. Little did I know, the night would be filled with images that I could always forget, and a feeling of happiness that would stay with me for a long time.

texts.full_text: rial court committing him to the Oregon Health Authority for treatment as a mentally ill person. ORS 426.130(1)(a). He assigns error to the trial court's failure to consider less restrictive alternatives to commitment. We affirm. The record shows that S.S. has a history of mental illness, including schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. He has been hospitalized multiple times and has refused treatment in the past. The state presented evidence that S.S. was currently experiencing psychotic symptoms and was a danger to himself and others. The trial court found that S.S. met the criteria for commitment as a mentally ill person under ORS 426.005(1)(a). The court also found that S.S. was not amenable to less restrictive alternatives, such as outpatient treatment or voluntary hospitalization. The court based this finding on S.S.'s history of refusing treatment and the current evidence of his psychotic symptoms. On appeal, S.S. argues that the trial court erred in not considering less restrictive alternatives. However, the record shows that the trial court did consider less restrictive alternatives and found them to be inappropriate given S.S.'s history and current condition. ORS 426.130(1)(a) requires that a person be committed to the Oregon Health Authority only if they are a danger to themselves or others and are not amenable to less restrictive alternatives. The trial court's findings are supported by substantial evidence in the record. Therefore,

texts.full_text: Bucharest, Romania – 11 October 2001, Fontenay-aux-Roses, France) was a Romanian-born French painter. Biography Dinu Grigoresco was the grandson of Nicolae Grigorescu, one of the leading Romanian painters of the nineteenth century. He studied at the Fine Arts Academy of Bucharest before leaving for Paris in 1939, where he won a scholarship to the National School of Fine Arts. He would spend the rest of his life in France. He initially studied under Léon Sabatier and Nicolas Untersteller, though it was the two years that he spent in André Lhote’s studio that most influenced his work. A member of the lyrical abstraction group, he moved in the artistic and literary circles of postwar Paris, befriending, among others, Francis Gruber, Jean Dewasne, and Constantin Brancusi. In 1956, he exhibited for the first time at the Salon des Surindépendants in Paris. Beginning in 1957, he also contributed regularly to the Salon des Réalités Nouvelles. France's Ministry of Culture purchased the painting that he exhibited at the 1974 edition of that salon for the Musée d’Art Moderne de la ville de Pof Paris. Grigoresco was married twice: first to Gabriella Haimovici, with whom his son Nicolae Grigorescu is a painter and sculptor; then later he would marry Jeannine Lévy. He died in Fontenay-aux-Roses on 11 October 2001. Artistic style Grigoresco's work was heavily influenced by the color theories of Johannes Itten, Wassily Kandinsky (whose "Composition VII" is one his favorite paintings), and Henri Matisse. His palette favored a limited range with an emphasis on earth tones; he used monochromatic schemes in many works as well. His style evolved from abstract to more figurative over time, but remained rooted firmly within the lyrical abstraction movement that was prominent during Grigoresco's early career

texts.full_text: p to Mumbai and had just come to my Swiss Junction Showroom after having my lunch at the “Lucknow ke Tunde Kebab” in the food Court of Mega Mall, Oshiwara. It was a wonderful afternoon and since I was at the store and it was a very dull hour for business…. I asked three of my Sales people to go for lunch and come back early. While composing a mail…..A familiar looking lady just popped in and ushered an 11 year old boy in and requested me to take care of him. I beinga friendly person, agreed and started chatting with the boy. He was full of energy and had a lot of questions about the watches we had on display. I showed him a few and explained the features. He seemed really interested and asked if he could try one on. I helped him put on a watch and he looked at it, his eyes wide with excitement. Just then, the lady who had brought him in returned. She thanked me and said, "He's been asking about watches all day. I'm glad he found someone who could explain them so well." I smiled and said, "No problem, it was my pleasure." As they were about to leave, the boy turned to me and said, "Thank you, uncle. I want to be like you when I grow up." I was taken aback and touched by his words. I ruffled his hair and said, "Well, that's a big responsibility. But I believe in you." As they left, I felt a sense of contentment. It was a small moment, but it made my day. I went back to composing

texts.full_text: over fifty should remember how the name Bobby Sands went round the world. The New York Times editorialized how Bobby “bested” British Prime Minister Maggie Thatcher because he and his comrades showed real moral courage as they fought for their rights as political prisoners with nothing but their bodies, while she just showed vengeance and spite. When Bobby Sands ran and won elected the Irish election from jail in 1981 it was a historic moment that gave Ireland hope of freedom. The prison where I attended this party is one among many across America with its own history, including some not so pleasant ones involving conditions for prisoners. But last week’s gathering wasn't morbid or depressing; instead we celebrated the life and legacy Bobby Sands left behind as a symbol against oppression in all forms. Bobby's story should never be forgotten because he was more than just another political prisoner – his hunger strike became an international call to action for human rights. It demonstrated that even when faced with extreme adversity, one person can make significant sacrifices and inspire others around the world. The birthday party at Ohio State Penitentiary brought together a diverse group of people who shared respect towards Bobby Sands' ideals – freedom from oppression in all its forms whether it be racial discrimination or political imprisonment. The atmosphere was festive but also solemn as we honored his memory, with speeches and songs that celebrated the spirit he embodied. Despite being imprisoned himself for years on end without any hope of release due to a conflict between Irish republicans like him who wanted complete independence from British rule versus those seeking an independent Ireland within Britain's borders – Bobby Sands never gave up fighting against what was wrong, even at great personal cost. His unyielding stance in the face adversity serves as constant reminder that sometimes one must stand alone for justice to be achieved. As we celebrate his life and reflect on this important occasion during which he would have turned sixty years old
